headline: AI's Real-World Impact on Jobs: Entry-Level Roles Softening While New Opportunities Emerge

stories.title: Stanford Economists Reveal Entry-Level Dev Hiring Down 20%
stories.summary: Stanford's SIEPR panel dropped actual data showing entry-level software developer hiring (ages 22-26) is already down 20% and call center jobs down 15%. This ain't projection—it's happening right now.
stories.takeaway: Young developers need to level up or pivot. AI jobs with heavy exposure are getting hit, but manual labor and healthcare roles are still holding strong.

stories.title: GenAI Reshaping White-Collar Work, Not Erasing It
stories.summary: Harvard researchers analyzed nearly all U.S. job postings from 2019-March 2025 and found routine roles dropped 13% since ChatGPT, but analytical and creative jobs jumped 20%. Companies are literally reshuffling the deck.
stories.takeaway: The real risk is for workers stuck automating their old tasks instead of learning to use AI. Reskilling is literally the move.

stories.title: Miami-Dade Launches AI Training Push for Small Biz & Public Sector
stories.summary: Miami-Dade County just partnered with Google's InnovateUS to offer free AI courses to public employees and small businesses. They're literally lowkey trying to make sure nobody gets left behind.
stories.takeaway: South Florida's actively investing in upskilling its workforce. If you're in the area, this is free professional development you can't ignore.

stories.title: CareerSource Broward Gets $300K Grant for AI Training
stories.summary: Broward County just secured major funding specifically for AI training programs aimed at local businesses. They're not messing around—this is serious investment in the workforce.
stories.takeaway: South Florida is doubling down on making sure businesses and workers get AI-ready. Job market's shifting, but the region's actually prepping for it.

watchNext.title: Healthcare Jobs Leading Growth While Tech Faces Headwinds
watchNext.why: Healthcare is literally the main job creator in 2026. If AI's squeezing tech roles, health positions are your safest bet—they're hiring and ain't slowing down.

watchNext.title: 227 AI Engineer Openings in Miami Right Now
watchNext.why: Miami's got serious AI jobs available if you've got the skills. Companies like Scribd, PWC, and DataAnnotation are actively hiring—fr fr, the market's hot for actual AI talent.
